exclusion_reason_key.prior_overlap: Substantially overlaps a story already published in a prior brief (either flagged deduped_from_prior:true in the digest, or independently identified by cross-checking prior briefs despite a false flag).
exclusion_reason_key.stale: Dated well outside the ~24-48h freshness window for a daily brief (multiple days to weeks old) with no evidence it's a developing follow-up; treated as a digest artifact rather than fresh news.
exclusion_reason_key.low_value: Minor/incremental product tweak or update, insufficiently substantive for a scarce brief slot.
exclusion_reason_key.low_value_redundant: Substantively similar to a stronger story already selected from the same organization/theme; excluded to avoid over-representation.
exclusion_reason_key.cut_for_balance: A legitimate, defensible new story that was cut only to keep the overall selection within the ~8-12 target size; a reasonable alternate pick if more slots were available.

candidates_from_digest.title: Fable 5 Moves to Usage Credits, ID Verification Policy Takes Effect
candidates_from_digest.summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 model transitions from subscription-included to API-only, requiring usage credits at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per output tokens. Government-issued ID verification via Persona becomes mandatory for access.
candidates_from_digest.note: Flagged deduped_from_prior; Fable 5 API-only/credits transition already covered in a prior brief.

candidates_from_digest.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Web and Mobile with Remote Session Support
candidates_from_digest.summary: Anthropic expands Claude Cowork (agentic tool for general knowledge work) to web and mobile platforms for Max subscribers, enabling users to start tasks on desktop and monitor progress on phone with work continuing in background.
candidates_from_digest.note: Genuine new product expansion (web/mobile Cowork) not seen in prior briefs; solid mid-tier products item.

candidates_from_digest.title: Claude Code Adds Manual Permission Mode, Dynamic Workflow Settings
candidates_from_digest.summary: Anthropic updates Claude Code with manual permission mode as default (requiring explicit approval for sensitive actions), dynamic workflow size controls, richer telemetry, and numerous stability fixes for macOS, Windows, and daemon management.
candidates_from_digest.note: Incremental Claude Code settings/permissions update — minor product tweak, thinner than other selected Anthropic product news.

candidates_from_digest.title: Claude for Government Desktop Launches Claude Code and Cowork in Public Beta with FedRAMP High
candidates_from_digest.summary: Anthropic launches public beta of Claude Code and Claude Cowork in Claude for Government Desktop, built on FedRAMP High authorized infrastructure, with tamper-evident audit logs, layered admin controls, and spend governance for federal agencies.
candidates_from_digest.note: New, high-significance product+policy crossover: first FedRAMP High public beta of Claude Code/Cowork for federal government; distinct from the earlier Alberta case-study coverage.

candidates_from_digest.summary: Google DeepMind scrapped the Gemini 2.5 Pro base model and rebuilt Gemini 3.5 Pro from scratch, targeting July 17 launch with 2 million token context window, deep reasoning layer, and improvements in math and SVG generation to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic.
candidates_from_digest.note: Flagged deduped_from_prior; Gemini delay/rebuild narrative already threaded through recent DeepMind-exodus coverage.

candidates_from_digest.title: Google DeepMind Loses Four Top AI Researchers in June Exodus to Competitors
candidates_from_digest.summary: Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead) departs for OpenAI and Nobel laureate John Jumper joins Anthropic, alongside Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel. Exodus wiped ~$225 billion from Alphabet's market cap as investors reacted to talent departures and Gemini delays.

candidates_from_digest.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Shipping on Cerebras Infrastructure at 750 Tokens/Second

candidates_from_digest.title: Mistral AI Enters July Early Access with New Open-Weight Frontier Model

candidates_from_digest.note: High-significance new model news: Mistral's next open-weight frontier MoE family entering early access; distinct from Mistral's funding news and Leanstral research release also selected.

candidates_from_digest.title: Mistral Leanstral 1.5 Reaches State-of-Art on Formal Verification, Solves 587/672 PutnamBench Problems
candidates_from_digest.summary: Mistral releases Leanstral 1.5, an Apache 2.0 licensed Lean 4 proof engineering model achieving 100% saturation on miniF2F, solving 587 of 672 PutnamBench problems with improved formal verification. Cost ~$4 per problem vs competitors at $50-300.
candidates_from_digest.note: New, concrete research result (587/672 PutnamBench, 100% miniF2F, ~$4/problem) — substantive technical news, not previously covered.

Time-horizon estimates range 11.3h to 270h depending on counting method.
